industries.name: Fundamental Nuclear & Atomic Research
uses: Named after Wilhelm Röntgen, discoverer of X-rays. Produced at GSI by bombarding bismuth-209 with nickel-64 ions. The 282Rg isotope with its 100-second half-life is relatively long-lived for this region. Predicted to have unusual properties — relativistic stabilization of the 7s electrons may give roentgenium gold-like chemical properties rather than silver-like (its lighter congener).
Longest-lived known isotope: 282Rg, t½ = 100 seconds. First synthesized: 1994, GSI Darmstadt (Hofmann group).

Research probes the limits of nuclear stability, tests relativistic quantum chemistry predictions in superheavy atoms, and searches for the theoretical island of stability predicted around Z=114, N=184.
All production requires specialized particle accelerators and isotope reactor facilities — this element cannot be made by any other means.

companies.name: GSI Helmholtz Centre
companies.use: Discovered roentgenium in 1994 in the same experimental campaign that produced darmstadtium, using bismuth-209 + nickel-64 cold fusion and the SHIP separator. The naming honored Röntgen's discovery of X-rays exactly 100 years earlier (1895–1995).

companies.name: Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
companies.use: Provided independent confirmation of roentgenium using the BGS separator, measuring decay chains that matched GSI's data — the confirmation required by IUPAC for official recognition of element 111.

companies.name: JINR Dubna
companies.use: Contributed additional roentgenium isotope data using hot fusion reactions with actinide targets, extending the known isotope chart for element 111 and providing complementary nuclear structure information.
